disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid deterioration over ~12 hours in a resident who was well yesterday, with high fever and shaking chills (rigors)
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and how quickly did it come on?
Has she had a fever or shaking chills?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She was completely fine yesterday. This all came on today — she's burning up at 39.2 and had really violent shaking and chills a few hours ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New acute confusion and marked drowsiness — a change from the resident's normal cognition
trigger_question_examples: Is she her normal self, or is she confused?
Can she talk to you normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, she's talking complete nonsense and she's very drowsy — only opens her eyes if you speak up loudly. That's not like her at all.

trigger_question_examples: How is her breathing — is it fast?
Can you count her breaths for me over a minute?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Her breathing is really fast — I counted about 28 breaths a minute, and it looks like hard work for her.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Low blood pressure reported on the facility cuff (85/50) with fast heart rate (115)
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to take her blood pressure and pulse?
What does the monitor show for her blood pressure?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The cuff read 85 over 50 and her pulse is about 115. It's much lower than her usual blood pressure.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Blotchy, mottled skin over the lower legs and cool-looking peripheries (poor-perfusion appearance)
trigger_question_examples: What does the skin on her legs and feet look like?
Do her hands and feet look pale, blue or blotchy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, the skin on her lower legs looks blotchy and marbled, and her feet feel cool.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Passed almost no urine for ~8 hours despite attempted oral fluids; strong smell of concentrated urine
trigger_question_examples: Has she been passing urine normally today?
When did she last pass urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's hardly passed any urine for about 8 hours, and what there is smells very strong and concentrated.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A urinary infection was diagnosed two days ago and she was started on trimethoprim, but she has continued to deteriorate on it
trigger_question_examples: Has she been unwell or on any treatment recently?
Is she on any antibiotics at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She was diagnosed with a mild urine infection two days ago and put on trimethoprim tablets, but she's just got worse and worse despite taking them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She will not drink and cannot manage her own oral intake because of drowsiness
trigger_question_examples: Is she able to drink anything?
Can she swallow safely at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She won't drink — she's too drowsy and we're worried she'd choke if we pushed fluids.
